Section.Text: Where any person designated by the family council of a deceased Naskapi beneficiary to receive the deceased’s traditional property pursuant to this Part renounces the property in question before taking possession of it, and no other person is designated by the family council within six months of such renunciation, the disposition of that property shall thenceforth be governed by the laws of the Province relating to intestate succession.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 187(1)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in subsection (2), a Naskapi beneficiary who is an Indian as defined in the Indian Act; and

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 187(2) of the French version of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
Pour l’application de la présente partie, sont considérés comme situés en permanence sur les terres de catégorie IA-N les biens personnels :
Paragraphs 187(2)(a) to (c) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that became the property of the band by virtue of section 15, as it read on July 3, 1984, and had been purchased by Canada with money appropriated by Parliament,

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: that is purchased by Canada after the coming into force of this Part with money appropriated by Parliament for the use and benefit of Indians or the band, or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: that is given, after the coming into force of this Part, to Indians or to the band under a treaty or agreement between the band and Canada

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 187(2) of the English version of the Act after paragraph (c) is replaced by the following:

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: shall be deemed always to be situated on Category IA-N land.

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 188(1)(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the interest of an Indian or the band in Category IA-N land; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the personal property of an Indian or the band situated on Category IA-N land.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 188(2)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: neither an Indian nor the band is subject to taxation in respect of the ownership, occupation, possession or use of any property described in paragraph (1)(a) or (b) or is otherwise subject to taxation in respect of any such property; and

Section.Text: Section 190 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Subject to this Part, movable and immovable property situated on Category IA-N land and belonging to a Naskapi beneficiary or an Indian ordinarily resident on Category IA-N land, and any right or interest of such a person in Category IA-N land, is not subject to privilege, hypothec or any other charge, or to attachment, levy, seizure or execution, in favour of or at the instance of any person other than a Naskapi beneficiary, the band or an Indian ordinarily resident on Category IA-N land.

Subject to this Part, movable and immovable property situated on Category IA-N land and belonging to the band is not subject to privilege, hypothec or any other charge, or to attachment, levy, seizure or execution, in favour of or at the instance of any person other than a Naskapi beneficiary, the band or an Indian ordinarily resident on Category IA-N land.

The right or interest of the band in its Category IA-N land is not subject to privilege, hypothec or any other charge, or to attachment, levy, seizure or execution, in favour of or at the instance of any person.

A right or interest in Category IA-N land of a person other than a Naskapi beneficiary or the band, or the immovable property of such person situated on Category IA-N land, is not subject to attachment, levy, seizure or execution in favour of or at the instance of any person other than a Naskapi beneficiary or the band, except where the band has authorized that person to pledge, charge or hypothecate that right or interest or immovable property, in which case creditors may exercise their normal remedies in relation to that pledge, charge or hypothecation.
A person who sells movable property to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a Naskapi beneficiary,

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an Indian ordinarily resident on Category IA-N land, or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the band

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: under an agreement whereby the right of property or right of possession thereto remains wholly or in part in the seller may exercise his or her rights under that agreement notwithstanding that the movable property is situated on Category IA-N land.

Section.Text: Section 191 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Property deemed situated on Category IA-N land
Section.Text: For the purposes of section 190, movable property

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that became the property of the band by virtue of section 15, as it read on July 3, 1984, and had been purchased with money appropriated by Parliament,

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: that is purchased after the coming into force of this Part with money appropriated by Parliament or by the legislature of Quebec for the use and benefit of Indians, Naskapi beneficiaries, or the band, or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: that is, after the coming into force of this Part, provided to Naskapi beneficiaries or the band under a treaty or agreement between the band and Canada

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: shall be deemed always to be situated on Category IA-N land.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 192(1) of the Act is repealed.
The portion of subsection 192(2) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Property deemed to be property of the Naskapi band
Subsection.Text: Where the band has the authority to delegate the power to coordinate and administer a program to the Naskapi Development Corporation and has so delegated, movable property that
Paragraph 192(2)(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: was purchased with money appropriated by Parliament or by the legislature of Quebec for the use and benefit of Indians, Naskapi beneficiaries or the band

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 192(2) of the English version of the Act after paragraph (c) is replaced by the following:

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: shall, for the purposes of section 190, be deemed always to be the property of the band.

Section.Text: Subsection 193(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A Naskapi beneficiary or an Indian ordinarily resident on Category IA-N land may, in writing, waive the exemption conferred by subsection 190(1) in favour of any person, on such terms and conditions as are agreed to by the parties, but, in the case of a right or interest in Category IA-N land, the consent of the band must be obtained to the waiver and the terms and conditions thereof, and the band’s consent must be confirmed by the electors of the band at a special band meeting or referendum.

Section.Text: Section 194 of the Act is repealed.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 196(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
The band may, with the approval of the Attorney General of Quebec and the provincial Minister responsible for municipal affairs, enter into an agreement with
Paragraph 196(1)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the Cree Nation Government,

Subsection.Text: Subsection 196(1) of the Act is amended by adding “or” after paragraph (c) and by repealing paragraph 196(1)(d).
The portion of subsection 196(1) of the English version of the Act after paragraph (e) is replaced by the following:

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: for the provision of policing services on its Category IA-N land.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 196(1.1) of the Act is repealed.

Section.Text: Section 197 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Every person who commits an offence under subsection 38(6), section 44, subsection 91(2), section 95, subsection 100(4) or section 108 is liable on summary conviction to a fine not exceeding $2,000 or to imprisonment for a term not exceeding six months or to both.
Subsection 199(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A by-law made under this Act may stipulate a maximum fine or a maximum term of imprisonment, or both, for contravention of the by-law, but the maximum fine or maximum term of imprisonment may not exceed $5,000 or six months, respectively.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 199:

In addition to the summary conviction procedures of Part XXVII of the Criminal Code, a proceeding in respect of a contravention indicated in the by-laws made under section 48.1 of this Act may be commenced in accordance with the ticketing scheme established by those by-laws.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 200(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Jurisdiction of justices of the peace

Subsection.Text: In addition to the courts and persons having jurisdiction in respect of the offences listed in paragraphs (a) and (b), justices of the peace appointed pursuant to section 12.4.1 of the Northeastern Quebec Agreement have jurisdiction in respect of
Paragraph 200(1)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: offences under the following provisions of the Criminal Code: section 266 (assault), section 445 (injuring or endangering animals) and section 445.1 (cruelty to animals).

Subsection.Text: Subsection 200(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

For the purpose of exercising their jurisdiction in respect of offences listed in paragraphs (1)(a) and (b), the justices of the peace referred to in subsection (1) are a summary conviction court within the meaning of Part XXVII of the Criminal Code.

Heading.Label: PART 3
Heading.TitleText: Transitional Provisions, Related and Consequential Amendments and Coordinating Amendments

Subsection.Text: The Cree-Naskapi Commission may prepare and submit to the Minister of Indian Affairs and Northern Development a last report in English, French, Cree and Naskapi, for the period beginning on the day that follows the end of the period for which the 2016 Report of the Cree-Naskapi Commission was completed and ending on the day on which section 98 comes into force, on the implementation of the Cree-Naskapi (of Quebec) Act, and the Minister shall cause the report to be laid before each House of Parliament on any of the first 10 days on which that House is sitting after the day on which the Minister receives it.

As soon as feasable after the report is laid before each House of Parliament, the Minister shall send a copy of the report to the Cree Nation Government, the Naskapi Development Corporation, the council of each Cree First Nation and the council of the Naskapi band.
The following definitions apply in this section.

Definition.Text: Cree First Nation has the same meaning as in subsection 2(2) of the Cree Nation of Eeyou Istchee Governance Agreement Act. (première nation crie)
Cree-Naskapi Commission means the Commission established by section 158 of the Naskapi and the Cree-Naskapi Commission Act. (Commission crie-naskapie)
Cree Nation Government has the same meaning as in subsection 2(1) of the Cree Nation of Eeyou Istchee Governance Agreement Act. (Gouvernement de la nation crie)
Naskapi band has the same meaning as in subsection 2(1) of the Naskapi and the Cree-Naskapi Commission Act. (bande naskapie)
Naskapi Development Corporation has the same meaning as in subsection 2(1) of the Naskapi and the Cree-Naskapi Commission Act. (Société de développement des Naskapis)

Heading.TitleText: Related Amendment to the Modernization of Benefits and Obligations Act

Section.Text: Sections 89 and 90 of the Modernization of Benefits and Obligations Act are repealed.
Subsection 46(4) of the Veterans’ Land Act is amended by striking out “or” at the end of paragraph (a) and by replacing paragraph (b) with the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: Category IA-N land, as defined in subsection 2(1) of the Naskapi and the Cree-Naskapi Commission Act; or

Paragraph.Label: (b.1)
Paragraph.Text: Category IA land, as defined in subsection 2(2) of the Cree Nation of Eeyou Istchee Governance Agreement Act.

Section.Text: Subsection 13(3) of the Access to Information Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (f):

Paragraph.Label: (f.1)
Paragraph.Text: the Cree Nation Government, as defined in subsection 2(1) of the Cree Nation of Eeyou Istchee Governance Agreement Act or a Cree First Nation, as defined in subsection 2(2) of that Act;

Section.Text: Subsection 4(2) of the Expropriation Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: No interest in land that is Category IA-N land, as defined in subsection 2(1) of the Naskapi and the Cree-Naskapi Commission Act, may be expropriated under this Part without the consent of the Governor in Council.

No interest in land that is Category IA land, as defined in subsection 2(2) of the Cree Nation of Eeyou Istchee Governance Agreement Act, may be expropriated under this Part without the consent of the Governor in Council.

Section.Text: Subparagraph 24(1)(a)(ii) of the Canada Lands Surveys Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: Category IA-N land, as defined in subsection 2(1) of the Naskapi and the Cree-Naskapi Commission Act,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii.1)
Subparagraph.Text: Category IA land, as defined in subsection 2(2) of the Cree Nation of Eeyou Istchee Governance Agreement Act,

TitleText.XRefExternal: Payments in Lieu of Taxes Act

Section.Text: Paragraph (c) of the definition taxing authority in subsection 2(1) of the Payments in Lieu of Taxes Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the band, as defined in subsection 2(1) of the Naskapi and the Cree-Naskapi Commission Act, if it levies and collects a tax on interests in Category IA-N land as defined in that subsection,

Paragraph.Label: (c.1)
Paragraph.Text: any Cree First Nation, as defined in subsection 2(2) of the Cree Nation of Eeyou Istchee Governance Agreement Act that levies and collects a tax on interests in Category IA land, as defined in that subsection,

Section.Text: Paragraph 78(3)(b) of the National Energy Board Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: Category IA-N land, as defined in subsection 2(1) of the Naskapi and the Cree-Naskapi Commission Act;

Paragraph.Label: (b.1)
Paragraph.Text: Category IA land, as defined in subsection 2(2) of the Cree Nation of Eeyou Istchee Governance Agreement Act; or

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 8(6)(b) of the Privacy Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the band, as defined in subsection 2(1) of the Naskapi and the Cree-Naskapi Commission Act;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 8(7) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (f):

Paragraph.Label: (f.1)
Paragraph.Text: the Cree Nation Government, as defined in subsection 2(1) of the Cree Nation of Eeyou Istchee Governance Agreement Act or a Cree First Nation, as defined in subsection 2(2) of that Act;

Subsection.Text: The reference to

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Loi sur les Cris et les Naskapis du Québec

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: in Part 1 of the schedule to the Specific Claims Tribunal Act is replaced by the following:

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemEn: Naskapi and the Cree-Naskapi Commission Act
BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Loi sur les Naskapis et la Commission crie-naskapie

Subsection.Text: Part 1 of the schedule to the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemEn: Cree Nation of Eeyou Istchee Governance Agreement Act
BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Loi sur l’accord concernant la gouvernance de la nation crie d’Eeyou Istchee

Section.Text: On the first day on which both subsection 19(2) of this Act is in force and section 352 of the Canada Not-for-profit Corporations Act has produced its effects, subsection 23(2) of the Naskapi and the Cree-Naskapi Commission Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Canada Not-for-profit Corporations Act does not apply to the band.

Section.MarginalNote: Bill — Impact Assessment, Canadian Energy Regulator and Navigation Protection

Subsection.Text: Subsections (2) to (4) apply if a Bill entitled An Act to enact the Impact Assessment Act and the Canadian Energy Regulator Act, to amend the Navigation Protection Act and to make consequential amendments to other Acts (in this section referred to as the “other Act”) is introduced in the 1st session of the 42nd Parliament and receives royal assent.
If section 10 of the other Act comes into force before section 131 of this Act, then

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that section 131 is deemed never to have come into force and is repealed; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph 317(3)(a) of the Canadian Energy Regulator Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: Category IA-N land, as defined in subsection 2(1) of the Naskapi and the Cree-Naskapi Commission Act;

Paragraph.Label: (a.1)
Paragraph.Text: Category IA land, as defined in subsection 2(2) of the Cree Nation of Eeyou Istchee Governance Agreement Act; or

Subsection.Text: If section 131 of this Act comes into force before section 10 of the other Act, then, on the day on which that section 10 comes into force, paragraph 317(3)(a) of the Canadian Energy Regulator Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: Category IA-N land, as defined in subsection 2(1) of the Naskapi and the Cree-Naskapi Commission Act;

Paragraph.Label: (a.1)
Paragraph.Text: Category IA land, as defined in subsection 2(2) of the Cree Nation of Eeyou Istchee Governance Agreement Act; or

Subsection.Text: If section 131 of this Act comes into force on the same day as section 10 of the other Act, then

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that section 131 is deemed never to have come into force and is repealed; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph 317(3)(a) of the Canadian Energy Regulator Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: Category IA-N land, as defined in subsection 2(1) of the Naskapi and the Cree-Naskapi Commission Act;

Paragraph.Label: (a.1)
Paragraph.Text: Category IA land, as defined in subsection 2(2) of the Cree Nation of Eeyou Istchee Governance Agreement Act; or
